purpose: Threats-to-validity and limitations matrix for benchmark paper review.

threats.threat: The simulator uses hand-designed demand, churn, reputation, risk, and cash dynamics rather than real startup markets.
threats.current_mitigation: Frame the benchmark as a controlled decision environment, not a real-market proxy; expose simulator code, score rubrics, market catalog, a human-calibration protocol, and a task-revision ledger for calibration issues.
threats.remaining_work: Execute expert review or human-founder calibration to assess whether task incentives match startup judgment.

threats.threat: The 50 public tasks are generated from 10 templates rather than curated from real startup histories.
threats.current_mitigation: Report task-family coverage, split balance, seeds, horizons, and action coverage in generated artifacts.
threats.remaining_work: Curate additional scenarios from public startup postmortems, accelerator cases, or expert-authored variants.

threats.threat: The public test split is visible, so agents or prompts can overfit to released tasks.
threats.current_mitigation: Label public_dev/public_test honestly and provide private-holdout blueprint plus evaluator protocol.
threats.remaining_work: Instantiate hidden task definitions on an evaluator host and report hidden-suite scores.

threats.threat: The current current release paper evidence includes deterministic baselines but lacks complete hosted/local LLM baselines.
threats.current_mitigation: Provider adapters, readiness matrix, submission validator, and experiment matrix specify exact missing runs.
threats.remaining_work: Run DeepSeek, Claude, Gemini, self-consistency, and at least one local open-source model on all 50 tasks.

threats.threat: Hosted LLM scores may depend on prompt wording, decoding settings, and stochastic sampling.
threats.current_mitigation: Support repeated-run reports, raw task outputs, audit mode, and submission validation.
threats.remaining_work: Report repeated prompt-sample intervals for hosted LLM submissions and freeze prompt versions.

threats.threat: A model can fail because of malformed JSON or provider errors rather than poor business decisions.
threats.current_mitigation: Count provider errors, parse categories, invalid actions, and over-budget decisions as diagnostics rather than discarding runs.
threats.remaining_work: Include redacted hosted audit traces for representative provider successes and failures.

threats.threat: The task-aware heuristic may encode task-family knowledge that ordinary LLM agents do not receive.
threats.current_mitigation: Report it as a capability-ladder calibration baseline, not as an agent-realistic baseline.
threats.remaining_work: Compare against prompt-only LLMs, self-consistency, and local open models to contextualize the heuristic ceiling.

threats.threat: Without a human or expert baseline, it is hard to interpret absolute scores as business judgment quality.
threats.current_mitigation: Use relative model/baseline comparisons, provide a human-calibration protocol and revision ledger, and avoid claiming real-world startup competence.
threats.remaining_work: Execute the protocol and collect small expert/human-founder pilot results or expert ranking of task actions.
